response:
a graphic design student is working on an augmented reality project where she needs to design a user interface that will appear correctly on various device screens. she is designing a button that will change size based on the screen's aspect ratio, which can be represented by the polynomial equation p(x) = 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7, where x is the width of the screen in inches, and p(x) is the area of the button in square inches.

the student needs the button's area to be at least 10 square inches for all screen widths between 3 inches and 6 inches. the inequality corresponding to this requirement is p(x) ≥ 10, and the range of x-values that satisfy this condition is 3 ≤ x ≤ 6. 

******  
the equation p(x) = 15 can be solved by setting 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 7 = 15. this simplifies to 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 3x - 22 = 0. the solutions to this equation are x = 2, x = 3, and x = 4. the screen widths that meet this specification are 2, 3, and 4 inches.
